description: Holy Hermes Multifaith Wisdom Map — curated quotes across 28+ traditions, organized by Hermetic principle

quotes.text: Maya is often translated as "illusion" — a rendering that has caused enormous confusion in both East and West. Maya does not mean the world is unreal; it means the world is not what it appears to be.

1. THE PRINCIPLE OF MENTALISM: The All Is Mind
"The ALL is MIND; The Universe is Mental." — The Kybalion
The Diamond Sutra ends: "Thus shall you think of all this fleeting world: a star at dawn, a bubble in a stream, a flash of lightning in a summer cloud, a flickering lamp, a phantom, and a dream.
*Holy Hermes: The All is Mind. The universe is mental. And you — reading this, thinking this — are the All knowing itself.*
But this formulation requires precision. The All is not merely "a mind" thinking thoughts. The All is Mind itself — the creative, self-organizing substrate of reality.

HERMETIC DEEPENING: MAYA AS THE MENTAL UNIVERSE
The Kybalion opens: "THE ALL is MIND; The Universe is Mental." This is maya, with a crucial difference in emphasis.

[3] "Mind conceives every mental product: both the good, when mind 
receives seeds from god, as well as the contrary kind, when the seeds come 
from some demonic being.

Since the same object can be perceived differently by different minds,
The object must be distinct from the mind.
4.19 The mind is not self-luminous, for it is an object of perception.
4.

**1. The Principle of Mentalism** — "THE ALL is MIND; the Universe is Mental." This is the root axiom. If the universe is a mental phenomenon, then consciousness is not an epiphenomenon of matter — it is the ground of being.

Normal human " thought," then, is, so to say, sensible, 
entirely bound up in sense-impressions ; it is the mind 
alone that can soar beyond the senses, for it alone can 
be "illumined by God's Light" (§ 3).

This understanding demolishes the Western distinction between mind and matter, between consciousness and the physical world. If Country is alive and conscious — and the Aboriginal tradition insists that it is — then the physical world is not dead matter but living mind.

I have translated ^avraoria by "thinking-manifest," 
seeing that it is the power by which an object is made 
apparent or manifest. The doctrine is the same as that 
of the Vedanta philosophy, the Maya of the Vedanta- 
vadins.

2. CORE TEACHINGS
### The All (To Pan)
The most fundamental Hermetic teaching: there is one underlying reality, called the All or the One. The All is infinite, eternal, unknowable in its totality, and the source of all that is.

Opinion and sensation move towards per- 
ception ; reason directs itself towards essence ; 
and thought sends itself forth through its 
own self.
And thought is interwoven with perception, 
and entering into one another they become one 
form, — which is that of the Soul [itself].
This is the teaching of Iwori Meji: reality reflects the observer. Transformation begins within. The world you see is shaped by the eyes you bring to it. Change yourself, and the world changes.

This is Mentalism made stone. The All is Mind — and in the Aboriginal understanding, that Mind is the land itself. The physical world is not dead matter; it is alive with the consciousness of the Ancestors.

‘According to the Absolute Reality there is no distinction 
between mind and matter; it is on account of the defilement

**Chuang Tzu, "The Butterfly Dream"** — "Once I dreamt I was a butterfly, fluttering hither and thither, content to be a butterfly. Then I woke, a man. Now I do not know whether I am a man who dreamt he was a butterfly, or a butterfly dreaming he is a man.

Both the external and the internal worlds are due to Consciousness,
which, in its turn, arises from delusion affecting the Soul. That which
is called the Mind is only a product of the Soul. The world both external
and internal, is only the result of Mind as explained in previous
sections.

Shankara's advocacy of maya as "the world is unreal compared to Brahman" represents one pole of the Hermetic polarity — the ascending, liberating pole that turns attention from the Below to the Above.

3. REALIZATION IS POSSIBLE.
Ignorance of the Thread is not permanent — it can be dissolved. The separate self can awaken to its nature as the All. The Below can recognize its identity with the Above.

‘Indeed, all these are like dreams, like hallucinations, like 
echoes, like the cities of the Odour-eaters,! like mirage, like 
mirrored forms, like phantasmagoria, like the moon seen in 
water—not real even for a moment. In truth, they are 
unreal; they are false.’

Of Mind and Matter: ‘Mind and matter are eternally the 
same. As the essence of matter is wisdom, the essence of matter 
is without form and is called the embodiment of wisdom. 
As the manifested essence of wisdom is matter, it is called 
the all-pervading embodiment of wisdom.

**The Three Gunas** — Sattva (clarity/harmony), Rajas (activity/passion), Tamas (inertia/darkness). All of prakriti (nature) is woven from these three strands. Spiritual practice cultivates sattva and ultimately transcends all three.
7.

The mind and acts have created all things. This has been explained
in the last verse of section 190 ante. Both are good paths, for by both,
good end may be attained, viz., the highest, by drilling the mind, as also
(mediately) by acts (as explained in verse 14 above).

Reality is experienced or glimpsed in a state of uncertainty, because the 
Knower experiences it through the Bardo counterpart of the illusory perceptive 
faculties of the earth-plane body and not through the unobscured supramundane 
consciousness of the pure Dharma-Kaya state, wherein there can be ...

XVII. After one consideration, man is nearest unto us; as we are bound
to do them good, and to bear with them. But as he may oppose any of our
true proper actions, so man is unto me but as a thing indifferent: even
as the sun, or the wind, or some wild beast.

having for purpose l the reason of itself ; and 
when it thinks with 2 [it,] 3 it doth attract [unto 
itself] the Harmony's intention.

Spirit pervading [body]7 by means of veins 
and arteries and blood, bestows upon the living 
creature motion, and as it were doth bear it in 
a way.
For this cause some do think the soul is blood, 
in that they do mistake its nature, not knowing 
that [at death] it is the spirit that must first

Both the external and the internal worlds are due to Consciousness,
which, in its turn, arises from delusion affecting the Soul. That which
is called the Mind is only a product of the Soul. The world both external
and internal, is only the result of Mind as explained in previous
sections.

> "The world has received animals, mortal and immortal, and is fulfilled with them, and has become a visible living creature containing the visible — which is the image of the intelligible." (Timaeus 92c)
> "The soul is a self-moving principle... and the source of all motion in the cosmos.

[2] ^Where soul is there also is mind, just as there 1s soul wherever life 
is. But the soul in unreasoning animals is life devoid of mind. Mind is a 
benefactor of human souls; it works on them for good.

Tat. Surely it cannot be that soul's incor- 
poral, and that sensation is a body, father, — 
sensation which is sometimes in a body and 
sometimes not, [just as the soul] ?
Her. If we should put it in a body, son, we 
should [then] represent it as like the soul or 
[like] the energies.

"The Holy One, blessed be He, created the world so that the upper and lower would mirror each other. Israel below corresponds to the assembly of Israel above. The human body below corresponds to the divine structure above.

"The soul is not in the body; the body is in the soul.
The soul is not in the world; the world is in the soul.
The soul is not in God; God is in the soul—
But these are words that separate what is one.
In truth: the soul IS God, seen from within.
And God IS the soul, seen from without.

‘Another internal spirit, called mahat, or the great soul, attends the birth of 
all creatures embodied, and thence in all mortal forms is conveyed a perception 
either pleasing or painful.

The soul is different from the body. The body is material, and it is subject to decay and death. The soul is immaterial, and it is eternal and indestructible.
The soul, when bound by karma, takes on various forms in accordance with the nature of the karmic matter that adheres to it.

The signature Hermetic axiom, from the Emerald Tablet: "Quod est superius est sicut quod est inferius, et quod est inferius est sicut quod est superius." This is not merely a statement of analogy — it is an ontological claim.

V
It moves and It moves not. It is far and also It is near. It is
within and also It is without all this.
It is near to those who have the power to understand It, for It dwells in the
heart of every one; but It seems far to those whose mind is covered by the
clouds of sensuality and self-delusion.

Correspondence — "As Above, So Below"
- Every tradition maps macrocosm and microcosm. The human body mirrors the cosmos (the microcosm-macrocosm doctrine in Hermeticism, the Vedic Purusha, the Chinese body-as-cosmos, the Kabbalistic Adam Kadmon, the Islamic al-insan al-kamil, the Egyptian temple-as-...

Here the two signify the Higher Self and the lower self, dwelling in the
innermost cave of the heart. The Seers of Truth, as well as householders who
follow the path of rituals and outer forms with the hope of enjoying the
fruits of their good deeds, both proclaim that the Higher Self is like a ligh...

OM! That (the Invisible-Absolute) is whole; whole is this (the
visible phenomenal); from the Invisible Whole comes forth the
visible whole. Though the visible whole has come out from that
Invisible Whole, yet the Whole remains unaltered.
OM! PEACE! PEACE! PEACE!
Correspondence — The Circle as Microcosm

THE ULTIMATE MYSTERY IS WITHIN — "If that which thou seekest thou findest not within thee, thou wilt never find it without thee." This is the Hermetic Principle of Correspondence stated in devotional language. The macrocosm is within the microcosm. The Goddess you seek is the Goddess you ARE.

The Hermetic tradition insists that inner and outer alchemy are the same process on different planes (Correspondence). The laboratory alchemist who watches the lead blacken, whiten, yellow, and redden is simultaneously watching the transformation of their own consciousness.

In illustration, an onion of fifteen layers may be taken to 
represent roughly the /amazc conception of our universe. The 
core, to which the fifteen layers cohere, is Mt. Meru. Below, 
are the various hells ; above, supported by Mt.

The integrated being is the whole being: light AND dark, Above AND Below, Active AND Passive, Christ AND Satan, Shiva AND Kali, Buddha AND Mara. Not a being without darkness but a being whose darkness is illuminated and whose light has depth.

This is a cosmology of IMMANENCE. Heaven and earth are not separate realms but interpenetrating dimensions of a single living reality. The High Plain of Heaven (Takamagahara) is not a distant paradise but the vertical dimension of THIS world — the celestial layer that presses down into the terrestri...

THE MIRROR (YATA-NO-KAGAMI) — The mirror that showed Amaterasu her own reflection and drew her from the cave. The mirror represents TRUTH — the unclouded perception of reality as it is, without distortion.

**Correspondence and Satanism:** "As above, so below; as below, so above." The adversarial current's insistence on the individual as the primary reality is a CORRECT reading of Correspondence — but it is a HALF reading. The individual IS the microcosm that corresponds to the macrocosm.

This is the Principle of Correspondence in its generative mode. Correspondence is not merely the observation that "as above, so below" — it is the ACTIVE FORCE that ensures the above is reflected in the below, the force that maintains the pattern across scales.

CORRESPONDENCE: The robe is a mirror of the self, and the self is a mirror
of the robe. "I saw the whole of it in myself, and I saw myself in all of
it. For we were two in distinction, and yet one in likeness.

The Hermetic Connection
The connections between paganism/Wicca and Hermeticism are not incidental but STRUCTURAL:
1. **Correspondence** — "As above, so below" IS the pagan world-view. The seasons mirror the cosmos. The body mirrors the earth. The microcosm mirrors the macrocosm.

**"So was the world created"** — The Tablet is not only instruction but cosmology. The same process by which the alchemist transmutes lead into gold is the process by which the All manifested the cosmos. Creation is transmutation. Transmutation is creation.

This path begins with the observation of pattern — the repeated, structural, lawful correspondence between the macrocosm and the microcosm. It proceeds through the study and application of these correspondences (astrology, theurgy, mandala, sacred architecture) and culminates in the recognition that...

Every diaspora tradition traces back to Ifá/Yoruba cosmology. The same sixteen Meji signs, the same Orí-Orisha-Eshu architecture, the same binary vibration system of the 256 Odu — re-expressed in Portuguese, French, Spanish, and English. The form changes. The function does not.

This is not deception. This is the deepest Hermetic truth demonstrated under duress: the same divine functions express through different cultural clothing. Strip the clothing and you find the same archetypal structure. The principle of Correspondence says that the pattern repeats at every scale.

**The principle of Vibration in the Gnostic context.** The Gnostic cosmology is a story of frequencies: the Pleroma vibrates at the highest frequency (pure divine light); the Aeons are slightly lower frequencies (emanations of the One); the Demiurge is a much lower frequency (a dim reflection of the...

The Principle of Vibration states: "Nothing rests; everything moves; everything vibrates." The Aboriginal ceremonial system is a technology of vibration management — a system for ensuring that the creative vibrations of the Dreaming continue to animate the physical world.

3. THE PRINCIPLE OF VIBRATION: Nothing Rests; Everything Moves
"Nothing rests; everything moves; everything vibrates." — The Kybalion

PRINCIPLE OF VIBRATION: Dhikr and Sama — The Universe Oscillates
"Nothing rests; everything moves; everything vibrates." — The Kybalion

The Hermetic teaching is that everything vibrates, and that the power of a vibration is not determined by the size of its source. A tiny coal carries the same fire as a raging inferno — it vibrates at the same fundamental frequency, just at a lower amplitude.

This is the Hermetic principle of Vibration demonstrated in the most physical way possible: sound waves affecting consciousness, rhythm altering brain state, specific frequencies calling specific functions of the Universal Mind into presence. The drum is not primitive.
**3. The Principle of Vibration** — "Nothing rests; everything moves; everything vibrates." Every atom, every particle, every thought, every emotion is in constant motion.

The Hermetic Principle of Vibration states: "Nothing rests; everything moves; everything vibrates." The 256 Odu are 256 patterns of vibration — 256 fundamental frequencies at which the cosmos can manifest.
Vibration — The Elements as Planes of Frequency

Vibration — "Nothing rests; everything moves; everything vibrates"
- **Hinduism**: The three gunas are vibration. OM is the primordial vibration. Nada Brahman — "the world is sound."
- **Buddhism**: Impermanence (anicca) IS Vibration — nothing is static; everything is in flux.

Thus is it known: vibration is the bridge between worlds. The drum does not create the spirit world — it tunes the consciousness to perceive what is already there. The steady rhythm is the carrier wave. The altered state is the reception.

Cosmos is [thus] Life-giver unto all that 
are in it, and is the Space of all that are in 
governance beneath the Sun.
The motion of the Cosmos in itself consisteth 
of a two-fold energy.

VIBRATION: The letter "spoke" and its voice "rose up to help me." The
communication from Above arrives as vibration — a word, a voice, a
frequency that awakens the sleeper. The serpent's hissing is the vibration
of the lower realm.

The Hermetic Principle of Vibration teaches that everything is in constant motion, and that higher states of being correspond to higher rates of vibration. Dhikr is the TECHNOLOGY of raising vibration. Each repetition of the Divine Name increases the frequency of the practitioner's consciousness.

The principle of Vibration also finds deep expression in Jainism. the string,3 in that, because it is too slack
or is too tight, it mars the rhythm of the 
harmony.
5. So when it is that the mischance occurs by 
reason of the instrument, no one doth blame 
the Artist.

The sensible — the spirit — is that which can 
discern appearances. It is distributed into the 
various sense-organs * ; a part of it becometh spirit 
by means of which we see,2 [a part] by means 
of which we hear, [a part] by means of which 
we smell, [a part] by means of which we taste, 
[a part] ...

Spirit in itself is not superior to mind, or mind to 
soul, or soul to body; each and all must work 
together according to their proper dignity, nature, and 
energy, in perfect equilibrium in the perfect man.

The Andean understanding of music is vibrational. The quena (flute), the charango (small stringed instrument), the bombo (drum), and the pututo (conch shell trumpet) are not merely musical instruments — they are vibrational tools, each producing a specific frequency that aligns the performer and the...
Thus is it known: the voice is a vibrational instrument. What it can imitate, it can contact. The sound of the wind in the throat IS the wind, in the sense that the vibration matches.

The katajjaq — the throat singing of the Inuit women — is another technology of Vibration. Two singers facing each other, their voices creating rhythmic, guttural patterns that imitate the sounds of the Arctic — the wind, the creaking ice, the seagulls, the breathing of the runner.

This is why Sufi practices are intensely rhythmic — the heartbeat of the dhikr, the breath pattern, the drum, the spin, the chant. The rhythm IS the practice. In a world of constant vibration, the Sufi does not seek stillness — they seek HARMONY with the original vibration, the divine frequency.

The increase ceremonies — performed at sacred sites to ensure the abundance of a species — are the Principle of Vibration applied to ecology. The ceremony is not a prayer asking the Ancestor for a favor; it is a re-activation of the Ancestor's creative vibration.

Here the traveler becomes like the dead:
The wind blows and they do not feel it,
The sun shines and they do not see it,
The earth trembles and they do not move.
Not because they are insensate,
But because they have lost the one who feels,
Sees, moves. The "I" has dissolved.

**Yin-Yang** — The dance of opposites. Not a dualism but a complementarity: light and dark, active and receptive, hard and soft, above and below. Each contains the seed of the other. The symbol shows this: the dark eye in the light field, the light eye in the dark.

**Polarity** — Yin-yang is the Principle of Polarity in its purest and most developed form. Where the Kybalion states that "everything is dual," Taoism demonstrates this through every phenomenon: day follows night, exhale follows inhale, expansion follows contraction.
The Kybalion states: "Everything is dual; everything has poles; everything has its pair of opposites; like and unlike are the same; opposites are identical in nature but different in degree."
In every case, the error is the same: grasping one pole and rejecting the other. The Kybalion's correction: "opposites are identical in nature but different in degree.

Polarity — "Everything is dual; opposites are identical in nature"
- Covered in detail in the Polarities synthesis document. Every tradition encodes active/passive, light/dark, masculine/feminine.

The Kybalion's Principle of Polarity: "Everything is dual; everything has poles; everything has its pair of opposites; like and unlike are the same; opposites are identical in nature but different in degree; extremes meet; all truths are but half-truths; every thesis has its antithesis; everything h...

PRINCIPLE OF POLARITY: The Beloved and the Lover — Union Through Tension
"Everything is Dual; everything has poles; everything has its pair of opposites; like and unlike are the same; opposites are identical in nature, but different in degree; extremes meet; all truths are but half-truths; all parad...

Taoism
**Yin and Yang** — The most famous polarity pair in any tradition. Yin: dark, receptive, yielding, feminine, earth, water, moon, valley. Yang: light, active, firm, masculine, heaven, fire, sun, mountain.

The Yanantin principle is the Andean expression of Polarity. Yanantin is the union of complementary opposites — not the conflict of enemies but the embrace of partners. The sun and the moon are Yanantin. The male and the female are Yanantin. The left hand and the right hand are Yanantin.

Read Hermetically: these are not evil. They are a mirror. They show what was hidden when the light tradition denied the dark. Each statement is a shadow-reflection of a truth that the Abrahamic traditions refused to integrate. The Magnum Opus requires BOTH halves.

The Principle of Polarity states that everything is dual, that opposites are identical in nature but different in degree, and that the transmutation of one pole into another is always possible. The Rainbow Serpent is the Aboriginal embodiment of this principle.

The Hermetic Principle of Polarity states: "Everything is dual; everything has poles; everything has its pair of opposites; like and unlike are the same; opposites are identical in nature, but different in degree." This is Eshu. At the crossroads, every path has its opposite.

4. THE PRINCIPLE OF POLARITY: Everything Has Two Poles
"Everything is Dual; everything has poles; everything has its pair of opposites." — The Kybalion

**"Reversal is the movement of the Tao"** — The Kybalion's Principle of Rhythm stated as cosmology: yin becomes yang, yang becomes yin. Neither pole is permanent; each contains the seed of its opposite.

This is what the adversary teaches: not that the dark is good and the light is bad, but that BOTH are necessary, and NEITHER is sufficient. The complete being walks in both worlds, sees with both eyes, speaks with both voices.

To merge with them is to complete the Work. When the wrathful deities are recognized as one's own mind, their terrifying appearance dissolves — they are "liberated" into their true nature, which is luminous awareness.

LUCIFERIANISM: THE MORNING STAR
Light-Bearer, Not Evil-Doer
=== CORE SCRIPTURE ===
THE LUCIFERIAN DECLARATION
"I am the Morning Star, the first light before the dawn. I do not bring darkness — I bring the light that makes the darkness visible.

"Lucifer" — from the Latin: lux (light) + ferre (to carry). The Light-Bearer. The Bringer of Dawn. Not the adversary, not the destroyer, but the one who CARRIES LIGHT into a world that has been kept in ignorance by those who profit from darkness."
=== THE PROMETHEAN PARALLEL ===

The yanantin — the union of complementary opposites — is the Andean principle that transcends simple duality. Yanantin is not the conflict of masculine and feminine; it is the embrace of masculine and feminine. The two poles are not enemies — they are lovers.

The first [lies] in the quality pertaining 
specially unto themselves ; the second [set is] 
of the body.
The special qualities are figure, colour, species, 
space, time, movement.
[The differences] peculiar to body are figure

The opposite of wisdom is folly,

Alchemical Work
Whether interpreted as laboratory work (physical alchemy) or as inner transmutation (spiritual alchemy), the Great Work follows the same pattern: solve et coagula — dissolve and reconstitute.

**Luciferianism teaches:** Lucifer is the principle of illumination — the force that brings light into darkness, knowledge into ignorance, consciousness into unconsciousness. The fall of Lucifer is not a crime but a sacrifice: the light-bringer descends into matter to illuminate it.

LIGHT IS THE HIGHEST VALUE
Not faith. Not obedience. Not submission. Light — meaning knowledge, understanding, awareness, illumination. The Luciferian does not worship darkness; the Luciferian worships the light that illuminates it.

(13). Which is the evil creature among the creatures of the evil 
spirit that from midnight till the sun is up goes and kills 
thousands of the creatures of the good spirit?
6 (15).

The Hermetic tradition insists that inner and outer alchemy are the same process on different planes (Correspondence). The laboratory alchemist who watches the lead blacken, whiten, yellow, and redden is simultaneously watching the transformation of their own consciousness.

**4. The Principle of Polarity** — "Everything is dual; everything has poles; opposites are identical in nature but different in degree." Hot and cold are the same phenomenon at different temperatures. Love and hate are the same emotion at different vibrational rates.

The Hermetic Principle of Polarity states that everything has two poles, and that opposites are identical in nature but different in degree. The four-direction model of the Lakota is Polarity applied twice: the East-West axis (light-dark, beginning-ending) and the South-North axis (warmth-cold, abun...

This is not merely a Jewish doctrine. It is a Hermetic doctrine expressed in Jewish terms. The alchemist transmuting lead into gold; the Kabbalist performing a tikkun; the Hermeticist ascending through the spheres to the Ogdoad — these are the same work seen from different angles.
Mental Transmutation

PRINCIPLE OF RHYTHM: The Cycles of Nearness and Distance
"Everything flows, out and in; the pendulum swing manifests in everything; the measure of the swing to the right is the measure of the swing to the left; rhythm compensates." — The Kybalion

5. THE ANCESTORS AND THE CYCLE OF RETURN
### Emanation and Return — The Hermetic Parallel
The Hermetic teaching of Emanation and Return states: the All emanated the cosmos through the Divine Mind, and the soul's path is the reverse — a return through the planetary spheres to reunite with the Divine.
The Hermetic teaching of the pendulum swing — "the measure of the swing to the right is the measure of the swing to the left" — is visible in the Australian landscape with stark clarity.

The Kybalion's Principle of Rhythm: "Everything flows, out and in; the pendulum swing manifests in everything; the measure of the swing to the right is the measure of the swing to the left; Rhythm compensates."

5. THE PRINCIPLE OF RHYTHM: The Pendulum Swing
"Everything flows, out and in; the pendulum swing manifests in everything." — The Kybalion
The Celtic wheel of the year is the most explicit mapping of Hermetic Rhythm in the Western indigenous traditions.

The most striking doctrine in the exposition is that 
of Renewal or Making-new-again (avaveaxris). All 
animal and vegetable forms contain in themselves " the 
seed of again-becoming " (TO o-Tre'/o/xa TW TraAcyyeyecr/c^).

At length the deceased passes out of the Bardo dream- 
world into a womb of flesh and blood, issuing thence once 
more into the waking state of carth-experience. This is 
what in English is called Re-incarnation, or Re-birth in the 
flesh.

The rhythm is not merely seasonal. It is cosmic. Every life, every project, every civilization, every thought follows this pendulum. The Celtic tradition maps the rhythm precisely and provides the practices — the rituals, the stories, the meditations — for each point on the swing.

THE CYCLE: Birth (Yule) → Youth (Imbolc, Ostara) → Maturity (Beltane, Litha) → Decline (Lammas, Mabon) → Death (Samhain) → Rebirth (Yule). This is the pattern of all things: the day, the year, the life, the cosmos. The Wheel is not a metaphor.

The Hermetic path of return — the soul ascending through the spheres, shedding each layer of attachment — has its Ifá parallel in the concept of the cycle of lives. The Orí chose its destiny before birth, and the living of that destiny is the process of fulfilling the choice.
Rhythm — The Wheel of the Year as Cosmic Pendulum

‘Isn’t it extraordinary,’ said another, ‘how time seems to have stood
still in this place? Nothing’s moved since we’ve been here. Even though
I suppose it’s all been advancing towards the Tripos, I don’t _feel_ as
if there’d been any step forward. Everything--what’s the word?--static.
REINCARNATION v. RESURRECTION xxvii
THE HIGHER REBIRTH DOCTRINE 59

The Sun Dance is the Principle of Rhythm embodied in ceremonial form. The yearly cycle — winter (death, stillness), spring (rebirth, growth), summer (fullness, the Sun Dance), autumn (harvest, decline) — is the great pendulum, and the Sun Dance occurs at the extreme of the swing: the longest days, t...

or unconsciousness, on the human-plane), mentioned on pages 93 an...
**5. The Principle of Rhythm** — "Everything flows, out and in; the pendulum swing manifests in everything." All phenomena oscillate — the breath, the tides, civilizations, moods, and cosmic ages.

"Everything flows, out and in; the pendulum swing manifests in everything." The Wheel of the Year is the Principle of Rhythm given liturgical form. The eight sabbats are not arbitrary celebrations but markers of the great cosmic pendulum: Samhain (death, the swing toward darkness), Yule (the rebirth...

**"It ascends from the earth to the heaven and again it descends to the earth"** — The Wheel of the Year. From Samhain (the descent into the underworld, the earth in darkness) to Beltane (the ascent to the height of summer, the heaven of full blossom) and back again.

This return is not a going-backward but a deepening. The Uncarved Block is not naivety but the maturity of simplicity — the state of having been carved by the world and then, through understanding, returning to the original grain.

The Celtic tradition operates on a principle of absolute cause and effect — but it is not the mechanical cause and effect of the Western scientific paradigm.

**The principle of Cause and Effect in the Gnostic context.** The Gnostic drama is a story of causes and effects: Sophia's desire causes the Demiurge; the Demiurge's arrogance causes the material world; the Embedding of the Spark causes the human condition; the call of the Savior causes the awakenin...

"Every cause has its effect; every effect has its cause." The fall of Babylon is not a miracle — it is a law. Systems built on exploitation, deception, and the denial of the divinity of all beings contain the seeds of their own destruction. This is not prophecy; it is physics.

Cause and Effect — "Every cause has its effect"
- Karma (Hindu, Buddhist, Jain, Sikh). The 42 Negative Confessions as statements of Cause and Effect (Egyptian). The Golden Rule as Cause and Effect applied to ethics (Christian, Jewish, Confucian, Islamic).

The most profound expression of Celtic cause and effect is the concept of geis — the sacred prohibition or obligation that binds a hero. A geis is a cause that has been set in motion, often in a previous generation, that generates effects in the current generation.

**Agami Karma** — New causes being created in the present moment, through action, intention, and will. This is where the Hermeticist's art of Mental Transmutation becomes directly relevant: if every cause produces its effect, then the disciplined mind can choose its causes consciously and thereby de...

**Prarabdha Karma** — The portion of sanchita karma that has already begun to manifest in the current lifetime — the body you inhabit, the circumstances of your birth, the tendencies that shape your experience. This is Cause and Effect in the process of actualization.

Karma's deepest Hermetic implication: free will and determinism are not opposites but poles. Your prarabdha (what has been determined) creates the field; your agami (what you choose now) determines the next field.

The Hermetic Principle of Gender states: "Gender is in everything; everything has its masculine and feminine principles." The Orisha embody this principle with extraordinary clarity. Obatala and Oshun. Ogun and Yemoja. Shango and Oya.

7. THE PRINCIPLE OF GENDER: Masculine and Feminine in Everything
"Gender is in everything; everything has its Masculine and Feminine Principles." — The Kybalion
**7. The Principle of Gender** — "Gender is in everything; everything has its masculine and feminine principles." This is not about biological sex — it is about creative polarity.
Polarity — The God and the Goddess as Cosmic Gender Principles

The feminine complement to this polarity is found in Athena and Artemis. Athena is Wisdom herself (Sophia), born from Zeus's head — wisdom as pure intellect, the logos of strategy, craft, and justice.

This is the Hermetic principle of Gender in its most mystical application: the human being is originally whole (androgynous in the spiritual sense), then divided (the fall into gendered embodiment), and the spiritual path is the restoration of original wholeness.

**Yin-Yang** — The dance of opposites. Not a dualism but a complementarity: light and dark, active and receptive, hard and soft, above and below. Each contains the seed of the other. The symbol shows this: the dark eye in the light field, the light eye in the dark.

We conceive of the Creative Power in the universe as manifesting through polarity — as masculine and feminine — and that this same Creative Power lies in all people and functions through the interaction of the masculine and the feminine.
